title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries · C Contract Brief: Memory Safety Properties
summary: A civic sensor team must distinguish a passing browser model from evidence that its native C release is safe on supported targets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem memory safety properties increment in memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.
Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Memory Safety Properties · Theory Memory Safety Properties
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8063. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
